turns.user: Mọi người đang bàn gì về GPT-5 trên Twitter?
turns.assistant_text:
Dưới đây là một số cuộc thảo luận gần đây về GPT-5 trên Twitter:

1. **@itsPaulAi**: "So Alibaba has released Qwen-3.7-Max… and it’s really good. You can connect it easily to Hermes Agent or OpenCode to basically replace GPT-5.5 or Opus 4.7. - 3.3x cheaper output vs Opus 4.7 - 4.0x cheaper output than GPT-5.5." [Xem thêm](https://x.com/itsPaulAi/status/2058612058581864715) (361 likes, 42 retweets)

2. **@rewind02**: "Kimi K2.6 topped every open-source leaderboard when it dropped. The honest verdict: best open-source vibe coding model available right now, not ready for complex production workflows." [Xem thêm](https://x.com/rewind02/status/2061067434044395593) (31 likes, 6 retweets)

3. **@goodworse**: "Opus 4.8 is just PART of the June MADNESS. This will be a REVOLUTION with upcoming models like Gemini 3.5 PRO, Grok 4.5, GPT 5.6." [Xem thêm](https://x.com/goodworse/status/2060266095798349903) (59 likes, 2 retweets)

4. **@0xKiyoro**: "> 20 free daily tokens cover a full working session on gpt 5.5 or glm. Most developers haven't noticed yet." [Xem thêm](https://x.com/0xKiyoro/status/2060047108451803542) (50 likes, 19 retweets)

5. **@kirillk_web3**: "🚨do you understand what China just dropped. Kimi K2.6 beats GPT-5 on almost every benchmark. Fully open source. 1 trillion parameters." [Xem thêm](https://x.com/kirillk_web3/status/2061370493920280723) (30 likes, 5 retweets)

Những tweet này cho thấy sự cạnh tranh giữa các mô hình AI và những nhận xét về hiệu suất của GPT-5 so với các mô hình khác.

items.title: So Alibaba has released Qwen-3.7-Max… and it’s really good.
items.summary:
So Alibaba has released Qwen-3.7-Max… and it’s really good.

You can connect it easily to Hermes Agent or OpenCode to basically replace GPT-5.5 or Opus 4.7.

- 3.3x cheaper output vs Opus 4.7
- 4.0x cheaper output than GPT-5.5

(And  2x cheaper input compared to both)

Everything you need to get started in post below

items.title: Kimi K2.6 topped every open-source leaderboard when it dropped
items.summary:
Kimi K2.6 topped every open-source leaderboard when it dropped

here's the honest breakdown after real-world vibe coding tests:

> Kimi 2.6 jumped from #17 to #5 overall on BridgeBench the moment it launched
> debugging: ranked #1. outperforms Claude Opus 4.7, GPT 5.4, and Gemini 3.1 Pro
> hallucination: ranked #3 across 175 questions
> refactoring: ranked #5. competitive with the best closed models in the world
> 262,000 token context window. $0.95 per million input tokens. for the tasks it's good at, the cost advantage is real

agent swarm mode is genuinely new: spawns specialized sub-agents - asset generator, game engine developer, gameplay developer - working in parallel on a single project

the part most people won't say out loud:

> the real-world UI output is noticeably worse than Claude Opus 4.7
> it adds features you didn't ask for. lava lamp test: instead of building what was requested, it invented speed controls, glow selectors, and color pickers
> Flappy Bird failed. Snake failed. Breakout marked destroyed blocks with an "X." these are not edge cases - they're standard vibe coding tasks
> benchmarks are being gamed. labs train specifically to ace the tests everyone watches

the honest verdict:

best open-source vibe coding model available right now

not ready for complex production workflows

use it for debugging. be careful with everything else

FULL BREAKDOWN AND LIVE DEMOS BELOW the most thorough real-world test of Kimi 2.6 I've seen since launch

items.title: Opus 4.8 is just PART of the June MADNESS
items.summary:
Opus 4.8 is just PART of the June MADNESS

this is the first of four planned models released in June

and it's simply AWESOME: it's not a lie, benchmarks have increased by +5-10%

coming up: Gemini 3.5 PRO, Grok 4.5, GPT 5.6

the labs are already promising insane benchmark gains

this will be a REVOLUTION

items.title: > 20 free daily tokens cover a full working session on gpt 5.5 or glm
items.summary:
> 20 free daily tokens cover a full working session on gpt 5.5 or glm
> opus 4.7 handles 2-3 serious architecture tasks a day at zero cost
> one GitHub account links to multiple Tembo accounts, the math on that is obvious
> 5 minutes to sign up, 10 minutes to your first merged PR
> no clarifying questions, the prompts have to be precise, the agent just executes
> the $100/mo tools and the $0 tool now ship the same output on most tasks

most developers haven't noticed yet

this isn't a discount, it's the same models, same capability, same output, for nothing

items.title: 🚨do you understand what China just dropped.
items.summary:
🚨do you understand what China just dropped.

a 16-year-old uploaded a 100-page PDF. got a full visual presentation in 2 minutes. ChatGPT couldn't handle it. Kimi K2.6 did.

here's the part nobody is talking about:
> beats GPT-5 on almost every benchmark.
> fully open source. 1 trillion parameters.
> trained for $4.6M vs OpenAI's $100M.
> runs on a Mac Studio at home. no data center needed.

China quietly changed the rules again.
while everyone was looking the other way.

the 7 free Kimi Skills guide is below.

turns.user: Tổng hợp tin AI trong tháng này.
turns.assistant_text:
# Tổng hợp tin AI trong tháng này

## Tổng hợp
- We examine how the expanding use of AI is reshaping the risk landscape for insurance–not only within the industry but across the broader economy and physical world. - [insurancejournal.com](https://www.insurancejournal.com/magazines/mag-features/2026/05/04/868016.htm)
- Adweek published a report on agentic AI tools in marketing, featuring insights from the ADWEEK House Possible Group. - [trendhunter.com](https://www.trendhunter.com/amp/trends/ADWEEK-House-Possible-Group)
- As 2026 advances, the digital landscape has evolved to be increasingly proactive and 'always-on.' Using technology to guide decisions is leading to the delegation of authority. - [forbes.com](https://www.forbes.com/sites/chuckbrooks/2026/05/08/agentic-ai-navigating-the-evolving-frontier/)
- Claims teams use AI-assisted tools to sort incoming materials, summarize large files and support litigation strategy. - [insurancenewsnet.com](https://insurancenewsnet.com/innarticle/genai-moving-to-the-forefront-of-claims-management)
- Benjamin Funk, Chief Technology & AI Officer at Acrisure, explains how the company’s implementation of AI at its core is key to its success. - [fintechmagazine.com](https://fintechmagazine.com/news/acrisure-how-ai-is-the-critical-core-engine-for-growth)

items.title: Emerging Risks to Watch: AI, Data Centers, and Autonomous Vehicles - Insurance Journal
items.summary: # Emerging Risks to Watch: AI, Data Centers, and Autonomous Vehicles. We examine how the expanding use of AI is reshaping the risk landscape for insurance–not only within the industry but across the broader economy and physical world. From cybersecurity and data privacy concerns to the infrastructure required to support AI, to its growing role in autonomous systems, understanding where AI-related risks may emerge is essential to managing them effectively. The wide-ranging–and growing–list of AI capabilities and applications makes it difficult to determine how the transformative technology may impact potential losses across specific insurance lines of business. A recently reported security incident involving data access from a popular enterprise generative AI tool highlights how the mass adoption of large language models (LLMs) and the concentration of the office software market may increase the potential for systemic liability exposure across cyber and professional and management liability lines.

items.title: AI Marketing Features - Trend Hunter
items.summary: Adweek published a report on agentic AI tools in marketing, featuring insights from the ADWEEK House Possible Group Chat co-hosted with Mutinex and participants from Hershey, Breeze Airways and PubMatic. The discussion focused on agentic AI systems designed to automate workflows, support measurement and connect large datasets with creative and strategic marketing tasks while maintaining human oversight. Speakers shared examples ranging from media-mix modelling that reduced data preparation time from months to hours to AI-assisted campaign workflows that remove repetitive tasks and allow teams to focus on higher-level planning and creativity. For marketers, agentic AI is increasingly positioned as a productivity layer that frees teams to spend more time on strategy, taste and creative judgment rather than manual execution. 3. **AI-assisted Creative Collaboration** — Tools that blend generative models with human oversight to iterate on strategy, tone and creative assets present opportunities to reshape agency-client workflows and redefine creative production timelines.

items.title: Agentic AI: Navigating The Evolving Frontier - Forbes
items.summary: # Agentic AI: Navigating The Evolving Frontier. As 2026 advances, the digital landscape has evolved to be increasingly proactive and “always-on.” Using technology to guide decisions is leading to the delegation of authority. AI systems that function with machine agency are referred to as "agentic AI." These systems can read intent, plan multi-step activities, use tools, access systems, and carry out tasks independently with little assistance from humans. The age of Agentic AI is transformative rather than incremental. Agentic AI is distinct from passive automation. This moment is characterized by operational autonomy and technical innovation. Agentic AI is increasingly establishing itself as the standard decision-making framework in critical systems. The implication is profound: algorithmic velocity is evolving into a form of national power. Agentic AI expedites the move to autonomous risk. Organizations must transition from reactive cybersecurity to "Security by Design" to mitigate these assaults. In terms of national security, AI autonomy will provide countries with considerable benefits in defense, intelligence, and cybersecurity.

items.title: GenAI: Moving to the forefront of claims management - InsuranceNewsNet
items.summary: # GenAI: Moving to the forefront of claims management - Insurance News | InsuranceNewsNet. HomeNow reading Property and Casualty News. Property and Casualty News RSSGet our newsletter. Claims teams use AI-assisted tools to sort incoming materials, summarize large files and support litigation strategy, while defense counsel uses GenAI to draft outlines, organize chronologies and prepare initial drafts. But for risk managers overseeing claims operations, the question is no longer whether AI should be used, but how to deploy it without introducing new operational, legal or reputational risks. Let’s explore how AI is being used across claims handling and defense, where it delivers tangible value, where it still falls short, and what recent cases and regulatory developments mean for companies managing risk. When it comes to handling and defending claims, AI is already proving most useful in three core areas: document review, case summarization and litigation support.

items.title: Acrisure: How AI is the Critical Core Engine for Growth - FinTech Magazine
items.summary: # Acrisure: How AI is the Critical Core Engine for Growth. Benjamin Funk, Chief Technology & AI Officer at Acrisure, explains how the company’s implementation of AI at its core is key to its success. For the modern business, AI is more than just a means to an end. Aside from the front-end benefits focused on efficiency, AI is adopting a vital role in every boardroom: strategy. Simply put, AI is more than a tool in fintech. It is rapidly becoming the solution to every company’s growth strategy. In this Q&A with FinTech Magazine, Benjamin Funk, Chief Technology & AI Officer at Acrisure, explains how Acrisure’s AI strategy is the core of its operation. At Acrisure, I’m now leading technology, innovation, and AI across our engineering, data, analytics, product, sales tech and agentic automation teams. Not as an overlay, but as a critical and core engine for how a business ought to run.

items.title: Emerging Risks to Watch: AI, Data Centers, and Autonomous Vehicles - Insurance Journal
items.summary: We examine how the expanding use of AI is reshaping the risk landscape for insurance–not only within the industry but across the broader economy and physical world.
